meaning: vt.解散,下课,开除,解职,使(或让)离开vi.解散
examples: He was so irresponsible that I decided to dismiss him.
I think we can safely dismiss their objections.
They were quick to dismiss rumours of an off-screen romance.
Based on that representation, the district judge denied a motion to dismiss the new complaint.
As a result of the settlement, Immersion will dismiss all pending litigation between it and Motorola.
